name: Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region (广西壮族自治区 / Gvangjsih Bouxcuengh Swcigih)
summary: The Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region (广西壮族自治区 Guǎngxī Zhuàngzú Zìzhìqū in Chinese / Gvangjsih Bouxcuengh Swcigih in Romanised Zhuang) is a Southern Chinese civil-law Chinese autonomous-region constituent unit of the People's Republic of China — structurally distinctive globally as the only Chinese autonomous-region established on the basis of Tai-Kadai-language-family Zhuang Bouxcuengh ethnic-identity framework (Zhuang Bouxcuengh comprise ~18 million population — largest non-Han ethnic minority population in China, speaking Northern Zhuang + Southern Zhuang Tai-Kadai languages distinct from Sino-Tibetan Chinese-language framework), as the only modern Chinese sub-national territory operating Romanised Zhuang script Gvangjsih Bouxcuengh Swcigih in official government framework (Zhuang Romanised script developed 1957 and revised 1982 — modern Latin-alphabet writing system distinct from rest-of-Chinese-speaking world's Chinese-character framework), as the only Chinese autonomous-region with international land border with Vietnam (~1,020 km Guangxi-Vietnam border + Friendship Pass / Hữu Nghị Quan historic Sino-Vietnamese border crossing framework), and as the central jurisdiction of historical Bach Viet 百越 / Baak-Yueq pre-Sinitic Tai-Kadai cultural-linguistic framework (~3rd millennium BCE - 3rd century CE Bach Viet framework spanning modern Guangxi + Guangdong + Northern Vietnam — pre-Sinitic Tai-Kadai cultural-linguistic substrate continuous through modern Zhuang identity framework). Guangxi historically operated under multiple frameworks including Bach Viet pre-Sinitic framework + Qin-Han Lingnan Commandery 214 BCE - 3rd century CE + Tang-Song Guangnan Circuit + Ming-Qing Guangxi Province, was established as Guangxi Province 1912-1958, and was formally re-established as Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region 5 March 1958. Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region comprises ~237,600 km² with a population of ~50 million (~18 million Zhuang + ~30 million Han + others). Family-law framework operates under the federal Chinese civil-law framework (Chinese Marriage Law 1980 + Chinese Civil Code 2020 Family Law portion Articles 1040-1118) applied uniformly across Chinese autonomous regions. The Guangxi High People's Court is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme People's Court of China. Guangxi is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. China is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Guangxi is governed by the Chinese non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of the People's Republic of China Article 4 (Regional Ethnic Autonomy)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of China Article 4
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Constitutional Article 4 providing for Regional Ethnic Autonomy framework — applicable to Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

statutory_framework.title: Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Federal Law of 31 May 1984 (revised 28 February 2001) establishing Regional Ethnic Autonomy framework — applicable to Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

statutory_framework.title: Zhuang Romanised Script
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Federal language reform of 1957 (revised 1982) establishing Romanised Zhuang script Gvangjsih Bouxcuengh Swcigih — modern Latin-alphabet writing system.

statutory_framework.citation: Chinese Civil Code 2020 Family Law portion (Articles 1040-1118)
statutory_framework.title: Chinese Civil Code Family Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Federal Civil Code 2020 Family Law portion applicable uniformly across Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

apex_courts.name: Guangxi High People's Court
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

apex_courts.name: Supreme People's Court of China
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

professional_regulators.name: Chinese National Health Commission (国家卫生健康委员会)
professional_regulators.role: Chinese Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

anonymisation_convention: Guangxi family-court decisions are anonymised per Chinese court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Qin Lingnan Commandery established
key_developments.description: Qin Lingnan Commandery established 214 BCE — beginning of Sinitic incorporation of Bach Viet pre-Sinitic framework 214 BCE - 3rd century CE.

key_developments.title: Guangxi Province established
key_developments.description: Guangxi Province established 1912 — Republic of China provincial framework 1912-1958.

key_developments.title: Zhuang Romanised Script Reform
key_developments.description: Chinese Federal Zhuang Romanised Script Reform 1957 — modern Latin-alphabet writing system framework.

key_developments.title: Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region established
key_developments.description: Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region formally established 5 March 1958 — establishment as Zhuang Bouxcuengh autonomous region framework.

key_developments.title: Sino-Vietnamese War
key_developments.description: Sino-Vietnamese War 17 February - 16 March 1979 — pivotal Guangxi-Vietnam border conflict framework.

key_developments.title: Zhuang Romanised Script Revision
key_developments.description: Chinese Federal Zhuang Romanised Script revision 1982 — refinement of Latin-alphabet writing system.

key_developments.title: Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law
key_developments.description: Chinese Federal Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law 1984 providing legal framework for Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region.

structural_findings: Guangxi operates a federal Chinese civil-law autonomous-region framework — places Guangxi in the Southern Chinese autonomous-region cluster.

Only Chinese autonomous-region established on basis of Tai-Kadai-language-family Zhuang Bouxcuengh ethnic-identity framework is structurally distinctive globally — ~18 million Zhuang population, largest non-Han ethnic minority population in China.

Only modern Chinese sub-national territory operating Romanised Zhuang script Gvangjsih Bouxcuengh Swcigih in official government framework is structurally distinctive globally — modern Latin-alphabet writing system distinct from rest-of-Chinese-speaking world.

Only Chinese autonomous-region with international land border with Vietnam (~1,020 km) is structurally distinctive within Chinese autonomous-region cluster — Friendship Pass / Hữu Nghị Quan historic Sino-Vietnamese border crossing framework.

Central jurisdiction of historical Bach Viet 百越 / Baak-Yueq pre-Sinitic Tai-Kadai cultural-linguistic framework is structurally distinctive globally — ~3rd millennium BCE - 3rd century CE Bach Viet framework spanning modern Guangxi + Guangdong + Northern Vietnam.
1979 Sino-Vietnamese War centred on Guangxi border framework is structurally distinctive globally — pivotal Sino-Vietnamese armed conflict framework.
Largest Chinese province-level division by Zhuang population is structurally distinctive within Chinese autonomous-region cluster.
editorial_notes: Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region jurisdiction sidecar — civil-law Southern Chinese Chinese-autonomous-region (Chinese Civil Code 2020 + Chinese Marriage Law 1980 + Constitution Article 4 Regional Ethnic Autonomy + Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law 1984/2001 + Zhuang Romanised Script Reform 1957/1982 + Qin Lingnan Commandery 214 BCE + Bach Viet pre-Sinitic framework + Guangxi Province 1912-1958 + Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region 1958+ + 1979 Sino-Vietnamese War + Guangxi-Vietnam border framework + Chinese non-Hague-1980). Only Chinese autonomous-region established on basis of Tai-Kadai-language-family Zhuang Bouxcuengh ethnic-identity framework globally + only modern Chinese sub-national territory operating Romanised Zhuang script in official government framework globally + only Chinese autonomous-region with international land border with Vietnam + central jurisdiction of historical Bach Viet pre-Sinitic Tai-Kadai cultural-linguistic framework + largest non-Han ethnic minority population in China.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Southern-Chinese + civil-law + Chinese-autonomous-region cluster + Tai-Kadai-language-family-Zhuang-Bouxcuengh-globally-distinctive + Romanised-Zhuang-script-Gvangjsih + Guangxi-Vietnam-international-land-border + Bach-Viet-pre-Sinitic-Tai-Kadai-framework + 1979-Sino-Vietnamese-War + largest-non-Han-ethnic-minority-population-in-China + Chinese-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
